title: Mainspring added wave turbine whose rotor is operating in waves
abstract: A wave turbine for converting sea waves to electric energy is, wherein motions; are transmitted from sea to land whereby a Connection Pipe (8) in which hydraulic fluid is filled with; of a Rotor (1) that is making both intermittent and even partial rotation motion in parallel to sea surface plane and in-waves plane around a Rotor Vertical Axis (3) that is vertical to sea surface plane. As for intermittent and partially circular rotation motion that is obtained being at land, is converted to continuously and complete circular motion by Land Machine (2) that basically has two pieces of mainsprings (18 and 19) and other secondary elements as well; electric energy is acquired by an Electric Generator (20) that Land Machine (2) also has.
claims:
Claims 1- The invention; whose Sea Blade (6) can rotate around Rotor Vertical Axis (3), directly in the waves and by waves' trindling; thereby being can convert wave energy to rotation motion by not a tortious way; at the same time, could being excluded from waves, its Counter Weight (7), which does not directly contribute to Rotor for energy generating from waves and which contrarily would create obstructive resistance to rotation, in the case of possible contact with wave; is being a single bladed Rotor (1); which can always be lifted, be lowered over Inner Sea Tower (5) against variable wave heights and so as to be accomodated to this and which is mounted on to this Inner Sea Tower (5); its special feature is; a Sea Blade (6), which optimally contacts to sea surface plane only with its bottom long side and whose upper long side as well optimally passes over upper limit of waves; thereby, making partially circular rotation motion in waves; in the sum of circular planes those are optimally starting from just over still sea surface plane to the upper limit, which is parallel and very close, created being by upper limit of waves; and; a Counter Weight (7), which is being positioned in all conditions as being in air; higher both than sea surface plane and than waves, by the reason of an angle that is given upwards on Sea Blade-Counter Weight Axis (4); and; a Rotor Vertical Axis (3), making rotation motion around of which; which can be lifted, lowered upon Inner Sea Tower (5), being having. 2- Determined optimal confrontation angle, between Sea Blade (6) with waves, each time about to contact with, for an efficient rotation motion being creating around Rotor Vertical Axis (3) as result of from horizon to land oncoming being waves' hitting itself, when looking bird's eye view, from sky to Rotor over the sea, is being of Sea Blade (6) of Rotor (1) in accodance with Claim- 1; its special feature is; might also with being some amount increased, decreased in compliance with the calculations and arrangements to be held; a 45 degrees of value being having. 3- Sea Blade (6) of Rotor (1) in accordance with Claim- 1 is being able to be brought back to its starting operation position -and to its first angle of meeting with waves- by bird's eye view, after each time of hitting waves' trindling rotating partially itself, in parallel planes to sea surface plane, by being rotated opposite direction around Rotor Vertical Axis (3); thus, being a Rotor (1) whose Sea Blade (6) can recurrently meet even next waves; its special feature is; a Reversing Motor (21); that is positioned on Land Main Axis (23); that first by 

 rotating Land Main Axis (23) opposite direction, and then, from land to Rotor (1), by transmission of this motion indirectly via Connection Pipe (8); that can carry out opposite direction rotation to itself; being having. 4- Sea Blade (6) of Rotor (1) [at the view looking from land to sea; from the conclusion of requirement of carrying out both two; both for the concern of standing (sometimes like this, vertical) in a 90 degrees of angle form -because of efficiency- against waves, when waves hit itself; and even for the concern of standing (sometimes, then, like this, horizontal) far from still sea surface and waves, for averting an undesirable resistance come into existence as a result of collision with other waves might later come, while being rotated to opposite direction, around Rotor Vertical Axis (3), for meeting with subsequently coming waves; this Sea Blade (6)] in accordance with Claim- 1 is being; which can be, around Sea Blade- Counter Weight Axis (4); which is, in a 90 degrees of working gap and which is, opposite direction to direction of coming waves; a rotatable Sea Blade (6); its special feature is; Sea Blade Hinge (9), that can rotate itself around Sea Blade-Counter Weight Axis (4); and; Sea Blade Motor (10), that meets its power from the electric generated from machine, that is positioned on Sea Blade-Counter Weight Axis (4); and; proper electronic operation programme that organises right operation times; being having. 5- Sea Blade (6) of Rotor (1) in accordance with Claim- 1 is being a very little power amount sufficient Sea Blade (6) for being rotated around Sea Blade-Counter Weight Axis (4), by the help of Sea Blade Motor (10), on the purpose of being sufficiently removed from waves and from sea surface plane; its special feature is; an extra Blade Side Counter Weight (11) which will balance itself by weight, against to Sea Blade-Counter Weight Axis (4), being having. 6- The invention, first, in circular planes, parallel to sea surface -and in waves- by making partially rotation motion, associated with waves' hitting and trindling; and then, for meeting next waves, this time making another opposite direction partial rotation motion around Rotor Vertical Axis (3), where it belongs, in consequence of an applied indirect power to itself, by Reversing Motor (21) on land; can be able to go back to its starting position and likewise, rotate also Rotor Vertical Axis (3), which it belongs to, in both two directions; thereby, can transmit, rotation motion obtaining over the sea, even besides opposite direction motion displaying Sea Blade's (6) both these two directional rotation motions, two-way and opposing, both from Rotor Vertical Axis (3), at sea, to Land Main Axis (23), on land and 

 from Land Main Axis (23), on land, on which Reversing Motor (21) is fixed, to Rotor Vertical Axis (3), at sea; thereby, holding two separate main rotation axes (3 and 23) of entire turbine together with being interaction; by this means, providing being able to install, greater amount of the machine parts which are in need of rotation, on preferable land, dry working environment, rather than installing at tough sea, salty water environment; lying over, inner or under the sea to land; is being a Connection Pipe (8); its special feature is; enough strength; and; full impermeability; and; suitable hydraulic fluid wholly filling in it; and; a Sea Blade Piston (16), which creates its sea-end; and; Land Main Axis Piston (17), which creates its land-end; being having. 7- Sea Blade Piston (16) and Land Main Axis Piston (17); who can provide obtaining linear transmittal motion, which Rotor Vertical Axis (3) at sea and Land Main Axis (23) on land, each of them, needs for bilaterally transmitting their both two-way circular motions to each other; being of Connection Pipe (8) in accordance with Claim-6 are; their special features are; a Sea Blade Rack Gear (14), that is fixed to the centre pivot of Sea Blade Piston (16), which creates sea-end of Connection Pipe (8); and; a Sea Blade Round Gear (12), that is being as making rotation motion together with this rack gear and by forward-back drive of this rack gear and whose rotation axis is created by Rotor Vertical Axis (3); and; a Land Main Axis Rack Gear (15), that is fixed to the centre pivot of Land Main Axis Piston (17), which creates land-end of Connection Pipe (8); and; a Land Main Axis Round Gear (13), that is being as making rotation motion together with this rack gear and by forward-back drive of this rack gear and whose rotation axis is created by Land Main Axis (23); being having. 8- The invention; [in which the energy of coming waves are after being converted to a partially natured rotation motion, that impacts on Rotor Vertical Axis (3) at sea, as a result of Rotor (1) structure, this time, similar partially natured rotation motion, that it has it made happen even on Land Main Axis (23), on land, to which it is transmitted being by Connection Pipe (8); rather than being orientated for rotating directly Electric Generator (20); because of Land Main Axis (23) is structured and positioned as being able to act as a mainspring winder axis, when needed; in which the motion is first orientated to the mainsprings (18 and 19), by Land Main Axis (23) and in which this motion energy is stored mechanically in mainsprings; in which complete circular rotation motion, that is essential for electric generating at Electric Generator (20) can only be obtained this way -because of mainsprings' ability of giving back the partially circular rotation motions which can be stored to themselves, by mainspring 

 winder axes, as being completely circular rotation motions- by means of being then sent from mainsprings to Electric Generator (20), for electric generating] is Land Machine (2), installed on land being; together with Rotor (1) at sea and Connection Pipe (8), as being one of the three main components of the entire wave turbine; its special feature is; Land Main Axis (23), which can make both two directions, partially rotation motions; and; two pieces of identical mainsprings (18 and 19); to whom such Land Main Axis (23) can store energy, to either of them by behaving as mainspring winder axis, while needed; being having. 9- Land Main Axis (23) of Land Machine (2) in accordance with Claim- 8 is being a Land Main Axis (23) which never makes energy loading to both of two mainsprings (18 and 19) at the same time [because of; mainsprings (18 and 19) having being individually central axes (24 and 25) and as result of mainspring structure; being both to the contraction rotation direction while a mainspring winder stores energy to them and even to the relaxation rotation direction while mainsprings give back their stored energy; being obliged to rotate opposite to each other -and two pieces in total- directions; and for this reason; mainsprings should have being spending any part of the time by rotating, to contraction direction, that is one of these mentioned directions and also other part of the time, to relaxation direction, that is the other of these mentioned directions; being even as is in this invention's running; when a matter of a continuous running need throughout the time stream is being; at least one piece mainspring that is required being to be able to give the energy back which is stored before to itself; that is able to rotate to relaxation direction always, at any moment of time, for supplying the required rotation direction for electric generation at Electric Generator (20)] ; its special feature is; a proper electronic operation programme which directs itself for loading to Odd Number Mainspring (19), at all times, the energy obtained being from -just as first, third, fifth, seventh, ninth, eleventh...- odd numbered waves; and; to Even Number Mainspring (18), at all times, the energy obtained being from -just as second, fourth, sixth, eighth, tenth, twelfth...- even numbered waves; from the mainsprings to which it will make one by one energy loading; being having. 10- The mainsprings (18 and 19) of Land Machine (2) in accordance with Claim-8 are wholly alternative in every respect to each other; are identical mainsprings being; their special features are; the same capacity for each with the other in terms of energy storage, the same physical properties and the same size being having. 

 11- The mainsprings (18 and 19) of Land Machine (2) in accordance with Claim-8 are; by regarding expected wave frequency at the region on which the machine is placed, average wave height, average wave energy and even Electric Generator (20) size which is used in the machine; besides, as a reserve, even by reckoning the possibility of Sea Blade (6) can not meet with the next wave for minutes, for unusual reasons; manufactured being mainsprings; their special features are; even each, individually; capacity of redundant than needed energy storage and operating Electric Generator (20) by alone, for some minutes; being having. 12- Axes (24 and 25) of mainsprings (18 and 19) of Land Machine (2) in accordance with Claim-8 are; on the same line and their neighborhoods, with Land Main Axis (23) which is located at the center of two of them [due to operation order of the machine necessitates from time to time, some of these axis parts (23, 24 and 25) to rotate together (same direction); from time to time as well, to rotate fully independent (different directions) and the joining need of neighbor axes of these together with, comes in sight for transient periods] fundamentally fully independent; discrete being; the special feature is; Even Mainspring Axis Lock (30) which interlocks both two axes together with, by immediately braking at Even Mainspring Axis (24) neighborhood side of Land Main Axis (23), in cases of Land Main Axis (23) should rotate same time, same direction with Even Mainspring Axis (24); and; Odd Mainspring Axis Lock (31) which interlocks both two axes together with, by immediately braking at Odd Mainspring Axis (25) neighborhood side of Land Main Axis (23), in cases of Land Main Axis (23) should rotate same time, same direction with Odd Mainspring Axis (25); being having. 13- Mainspring axes (24 and 25) of Land Machine (2) in accordance with Claim-8 are mainspring axes (24 and 25) being able to standing without rotating any direction, during operation of the machine and the time gaps in which the energy that was stored in mainsprings should sometimes stay without releasing; without using; their special features are; Even Mainspring Brake (28) on Even Mainspring Axis (24); and; as Odd Mainspring Brake (29) on Odd Mainspring Axis (25) as well; which are able to stop their rotation motions completely, by applying braking themselves; being having. 14- Mainspring axes (24 and 25) of Land Machine (2) in accordance with Claim-8 are being having the shape by which their both two directional rotating and staying without rotating motions exactly and ultimately can being conducted to Electric Generator (20); their special 

 features are; as being at the center and themselves being as rotation axes; Even Mainspring Wheel (26) of Even Mainspring Axis (24); and; Odd Mainspring Wheel (27) of Odd Mainspring Axis (25); structured as circular gears; the diameters of both two are same as each other and whose diameter is greater being than the largest circumstance of the mainsprings; positioned as vertical to the earth's plane and parallel to each other; being having. 15- A rotation pivot; to whose itself, mainspring wheels (26 and 27) come into contact with by touching over Even Pivot Gear (34) and Odd Pivot Gear (35); which creates central rotation axes of Even Pivot Gear (34) and Odd Pivot Gear (35); which cross over outside the circumferences of mainspring wheels (26 and 27); to which both two directional rotation and staying without rotation motions of mainspring wheels (26 and 27) are conducted, as a result of being in touch with the mainspring wheels (26 and 27) through Even Pivot Gear (34) and Odd Pivot Gear (35); of Land Machine (2) in accordance with Claim-8 is being; its special feature is; being about Even Side Pivot (32) at Even Mainspring Wheel (26) side; and; Odd Side Pivot (33) at Odd Mainspring Wheel (27) side; two parts being having. 16- Differential Even Pivot (38) and Differential Odd Pivot (39) of Land Machine (2); when Even Side Pivot (32) and Odd Side Pivot (33) stay both at two peripherals; then, these having the position lying being from inner-sideways to the center, on the same line and followingly Even and Odd Side Pivots (32 and 33) at both two peripherals; altogether creating being a longer rotation pivot are being; their special features are; a Differential Gearbox (42), to which they are connected with, at their central joint (just like the axis pivots of motor vehicles join to the differential gearbox from both two sides), which makes them possible to be able to rotate different directions in case of need, or, to be able to rotate for the other, while one of them is not rotating; and; Ratchet Gears (36 and 37) one for each of them, at the neighborhoods of them with Even and Odd Side Pivots (32 and 33), with whom they stay together with on the same line; and; Brakes (40 and 41) one for each of them as well, on themselves, on the purpose of might being stopped, in case of need; being having. 17- Differential Gearbox (42) of Land Machine (2) in accordance with Claim-8 is being; its special feature is; Last Axle (43) which provides its connection and motion conduction with Generator Gear (44), which is a belonging part of Electric Generator; being having. 

 18- Electronic operation system; which provides Rotor (1) in accordance with Claim- 1 and Land Machine (2) in accordance with Claim- 8 operating together and in compatible with, during the time gap while from the moment of an odd numbered wave hits Sea Blade (6) till the ending of rotation motion, which for this reason occurs at Sea Blade ; is being; its special feature is; all the programs of, making braking to Odd Mainspring Axis Lock (31); and; staying Odd Mainspring Brake (29) open, without braking; and; making braking to Differential Odd Brake (41); and; staying Even Mainspring Axis Lock (30) open, without braking; and; staying Even Mainspring Brake (28) open, without braking; and; staying Differential Even Brake (40) open, without braking; being having. 19- Electronic operation system; which provides Rotor (1) in accordance with Claim- 1 and Land Machine (2) in accordance with Claim- 8 operating together and in compatible with, during the time gap while after the motion at Sea Blade (6) arising from odd numbered wave and before meeting with even numbered wave; is being; its special feature is; all the programs of, staying Even Mainspring Axis Lock (30) open, without braking; and; staying Even Mainspring Brake (28) open, without braking; and; staying Differential Even Brake (40) open, without braking; and; making braking to Differential Odd Brake (41); and; running Reversing Motor (21) for getting Sea Blade (6) back to its suitable meeting position with waves; and; staying Odd Mainspring Axis Lock (31) open, without braking; and; making braking to Odd Mainspring Brake (29); being having. 20- Electronic operation system; which provides Rotor (1) in accordance with Claim- 1 and Land Machine (2) in accordance with Claim- 8 operating together and in compatible with, during the time gap while from the moment of an even numbered wave hits Sea Blade (6) till the ending of rotation motion, which for this reason occurs at Sea Blade; is being; its special feature is; all the programs of, making braking to Even Mainspring Axis Lock (30); and; staying Even Mainspring Brake (28) open, without braking; and; making braking to Differential Even Brake (40); and; staying Odd Mainspring Axis Lock (31) open, without braking; and; staying Odd Mainspring Brake (29) open, without braking; and; staying Differential Odd Brake (41) open, without braking; being having. 21- Electronic operation system; which provides Rotor (1) in accordance with Claim- 1 and Land Machine (2) in accordance with Claim- 8 operating together and in compatible with, during the time gap while after the motion at Sea Blade (6) arising from even numbered wave 

 and before meeting with odd numbered wave; is being; its special feature is; all the programs of, staying Odd Mainspring Axis Lock (31) open, without braking; and; staying Odd Mainspring Brake (29) open, without braking; and; staying Differential Odd Brake (41) open, without braking; and; making braking to Differential Even Brake (40); and; running Reversing Motor (21) for getting Sea Blade (6) back to its suitable meeting position with waves; and; staying Even Mainspring Axis Lock (30) open, without braking; and; making braking to Even Mainspring Brake (28); being having.
